topic: help with a Google Business profile

turns.content: alright I need help with something. somebody told me I need a Google Business profile. I looked it up and got confused

Yeah, Google Business Profile is basically your shop's listing on Google. When someone searches for tire shops or auto repair near them, that profile is what shows up with your hours, address, phone number, reviews, and all that. It's free and it's one of the best ways to get new customers. Have you started setting one up, or are you at square one?
square one. I think theres something already there because when you google my shop some info comes up but I never set it up

That's common — Google sometimes auto-generates a listing from public records. The good news is you can claim it and take control. Go to google.com/business and search for your shop name and address. If it shows up, there should be an option to claim it. Google will verify you're the owner, usually by sending a postcard to your shop address or calling your phone number with a code.
they send a postcard? like in the mail?

Yeah, sometimes. It's one of the ways they confirm you're actually at that address. The postcard has a verification code on it that you type in online. It usually arrives in about five days. Sometimes they'll let you verify by phone call or text instead, which is faster. It depends on what options come up when you go through the process.
alright. so once I claim it then what. what do I actually put on there

Once you're verified, fill out everything you can. Here's what matters most:

- Business name, address, and phone number — make sure they're exactly right
- Your hours of operation, including any days you're closed
- Business category — pick "tire shop" as primary, and you can add secondary ones like "auto repair shop"
- A description of your services, just a few sentences about what you do
- Photos — pictures of your shop, your bays, your team. Listings with photos get way more clicks

That covers the basics and will put you ahead of a lot of small shops.
photos. man I dont have any good photos of the shop

You don't need anything professional. Just take some with your phone. A shot of the front of the building, a clean bay with a car on the lift, your sign, maybe your team standing out front. Natural and real beats polished stock photos for a local business. Customers want to see what the place actually looks like before they show up.
I guess I could do that. what about reviews. I see some shops with like 200 reviews how do they get that many

Most of them just ask. After you finish a job and the customer's happy, say something like "hey if you get a chance, leave us a review on Google — it really helps." You'd be surprised how many people will do it if you just ask. Some shops put a little card with a QR code in the bag with the receipt. You don't need 200 reviews to start — even 15 or 20 solid ones will make a difference.
QR code. youre losing me now

Ha, forget the QR code for now. Just ask people in person. When a customer pays and seems happy, just say "I'd appreciate it if you left us a Google review." Keep it simple. That alone will get you rolling. You can figure out the fancy stuff later.
alright that I can do. so basically claim the listing, fill it out, take some pictures, ask for reviews

That's it. You just summed up the whole playbook. Claim it, fill it out completely, add a few photos, and start asking happy customers for reviews. Do those four things and you'll show up way better in local searches. It's free advertising that works around the clock.
free I like. alright ill try to mess with it this weekend. if I get stuck ill come back and ask
Sounds like a plan. It's pretty straightforward once you get past the claiming part. Come back anytime if you hit a wall.
